identificationModule.briefTitle: The PRIMO Study: Paricalcitol Capsules Benefits Renal Failure Induced Cardiac Morbidity in Subjects With Chronic Kidney Disease Stage 3/4
identificationModule.officialTitle: The PRIMO Study: Paricalcitol Capsules Benefits in Renal Failure Induced Cardiac Morbidity in Subjects With Chronic Kidney Disease Stage 3/4

descriptionModule.briefSummary: To evaluate the effects of paricalcitol capsules on cardiac structure and function over 48 weeks in patients with Stage 3/4 chronic kidney disease (CKD) who had left ventricular hypertrophy (LVH).
descriptionModule.detailedDescription:
Patients who met the inclusion criteria and did not meet any of the exclusion criteria were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to each treatment group to receive paricalcitol capsules or placebo. A stratified randomization scheme was used to ensure balance among treatment groups with respect to country, gender, and baseline renin angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS) inhibitor use (yes/no).

Participants who completed the 48-Week Treatment Period could continue on in the ongoing Long-term Follow-up Period that was to last 18 months, with study visits at 6 months, 12 months and 18 months post Treatment Week 48 Visit. Participants did not receive study drug, nor were they to have undergone echocardiogram/MRI procedures during the Long-term Follow-up Period.

armGroups.label: Paricalcitol
armGroups.description: Participants received paricalcitol capsules 2 µg once a day (two 1 µg paricalcitol capsules), for up to 48 weeks. Participants who completed the 48-week Treatment Period could continue in the Long-term Follow-up Period for an additional 18 months. Participants did not receive study drug during the Long-term Follow-up Period.

armGroups.label: Placebo
armGroups.description: Participants received 2 placebo capsules once a day for up to 48 weeks. Participants who completed the 48-week Treatment Period could continue in the Long-term Follow-up Period for an additional 18 months. Participants did not receive study drug during the Long-term Follow-up Period.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Change From Baseline in Left Ventricular Mass Index (LVMI) Over 48 Weeks Measured by Cardiac Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
primaryOutcomes.description: The Central Cardiac MRI Core Laboratory (CCL) interpreted and analyzed all cardiac MRI data. Left Ventricular Mass (LVM) was normalized to the participant's height by the following equation to obtain LVMI: LVM (grams) divided by height (meters)\^2.7.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Diastolic Mitral Annular Relaxation Velocity (E')
secondaryOutcomes.description: Diastolic mitral annular relaxation velocity (lateral E wave velocity; E') is a measure of diastolic function.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Ratio of Peak E Wave Velocity to Lateral E Wave Velocity (E/E')
secondaryOutcomes.description: The ratio of peak E wave velocity to lateral E wave velocity (E/E') is a measure of diastolic function.
E-wave deceleration time (DT) is a measure of diastolic function.
Isovolumetric relaxation time (IVRT) is a measure of diastolic function.
Left atrial volume is a measure of diastolic function.
Plasma triiodothyronine (T3) is a biological and inflammatory marker.
Interleukin-6 (IL-6) is a biological and inflammatory marker.
Troponin-T is a biological and inflammatory marker.
B-type natriuretic peptide (BNP) is a biological and inflammatory marker.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in High Sensitivity C-reactive Protein (hsCRP)
secondaryOutcomes.description: High sensitivity C-reactive protein (hsCRP) is a biological and inflammatory marker.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Progression of Thoraco-abdominal Aortic Plaque Volume
secondaryOutcomes.description: Change from baseline to Week 48 in thoraco-abdominal aortic plaque volume.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Progression of Thoraco-abdominal Aortic Wall Volume
secondaryOutcomes.description: Change from baseline to Week 48 in thoraco-abdominal aortic wall volume

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Progression of Aortic Compliance
secondaryOutcomes.description: Change from baseline to Week 48 in aortic compliance.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Progression of Left Ventricular End-systolic Volume Index
secondaryOutcomes.description: Change from baseline to Week 48 in left ventricular end-systolic volume index.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Progression of Left Ventricular End-diastolic Volume Index
secondaryOutcomes.description: Change from baseline to Week 48 in left ventricular end-diastolic volume index.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Change in Progression of Left Ventricular Ejection Fraction
secondaryOutcomes.description: Change from baseline to Week 48 in left ventricular ejection fraction.

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Estimated glomerular filtration rate (GFR) between 15-60 mL/min/1.73 m\^2
* Serum intact parathyroid hormone (iPTH) value between 50-300 pg/mL
* Corrected serum calcium level 8.0-10.0 mg/dL (2.0-2.5 mmol/L)
* Phosphorous level less than or equal to 5.2 mg/dL (1.68 mmol/L)
* Serum albumin greater than or equal to 3.0 g/dL (30 g/L)
* Echocardiogram results of:

  * Females: Left ventricular (LV) ejection fraction greater than or equal to 50% and septal wall thickness between 11-17 mm; and,
  * Males: LV ejection fraction greater than or equal to 50% and septal wall thickness between 12-18 mm
* If the subject is receiving renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS) inhibitors the dose must have been stable for greater than one month prior to the Screening Period. However, the subject may have switched to different brands but at equivalent doses as determined by the study physician during the month prior to the Screening Period.
* Subject must have a technically adequate baseline cardiac magnetic resonance imaging (MRI).

Exclusion Criteria:

* Subject has previously been on active vitamin D therapy within the four weeks prior to the Screening Period
* Pregnant or lactating females
* Subject is expected to initiate renal replacement therapy within one year
* Subject is taking calcitonin, bisphosphonates, cinacalcet, glucocorticoids (except topical or inhaled glucocorticoids)
* Subject had clinically significant coronary artery disease (CAD) within 3 months prior to the Screening Period, defined as either hospitalization for myocardial infarction (MI) or unstable angina; new onset angina with positive functional study or coronary angiogram revealing stenosis; or coronary revascularization procedure.
* Subject had major cardiac valve abnormality linked with LVH and/or diastolic dysfunction, defined as either aortic valve area ≤ 1.5 cm\^2 or a mean gradient of \> 20 mmHg; or regurgitation lesions; more than moderate mitral regurgitation, or more than moderate aortic regurgitation.
* Subject had asymmetric septal hypertrophy defined as septal wall thickness/posterior wall thickness ratio \> 1.5 based on screening echocardiogram.
* Subject had a severe cerebrovascular accident (CVA) within the last 3 months (e.g., hemorrhagic) prior to screening.
* Subject had full remission from a malignancy for less than 1 year except completely excised non-melanoma skin cancer (e.g., basal or squamous carcinoma) or any history of bone metastasis.
* Subject had comorbid conditions (e.g., advanced malignancy, advanced liver disease) with a life expectancy less than 1 year.

participantFlowModule.recruitmentDetails: Participants were enrolled in the study at US and ex-US investigative sites. Recruitment began in March 2008 and ended in December 2009. The study population consisted of participants with Stage 3/4 chronic kidney disease who had a diagnosis of left ventricular hypertrophy confirmed by echocardiogram and cardiac magnetic resonance imaging.

groups.description: Participants received paricalcitol capsules 2 µg once a day (two 1 µg paricalcitol capsules), for up to 48 weeks. Participants did not receive study drug during the Long-term Follow-up Period.

adverseEventsModule.timeFrame: All AE(s) reported from the time of study drug administration until 30 days after last study drug administration were collected (up to 52 weeks). In addition, serious adverse events were collected throughout the 18-month Long-term Follow-up Period.
adverseEventsModule.description: AEs were coded using the MedDRA version 14.0 coding dictionary for AEs collected during the Treatment Period and version 14.1 for AEs collected during the Long-term Follow-up Period.

eventGroups.title: Paricalcitol: Treatment Period
eventGroups.description: Participants received paricalcitol capsules 2 µg once a day (two 1 µg paricalcitol capsules), for up to 48 weeks.

eventGroups.title: Placebo: Treatment Period
eventGroups.description: Participants received 2 placebo capsules once a day for up to 48 weeks.

eventGroups.title: Paricalcitol: Long-term Follow-up
eventGroups.description: Participants who received paricalcitol and completed the 48-week Treatment Period could continue in the Long-term Follow-up Period for an additional 18 months. Participants did not receive study drug during the Long-term Follow-up Period.

eventGroups.title: Placebo: Long-term Follow-up
eventGroups.description: Participants who received placebo and completed the 48-week Treatment Period could continue in the Long-term Follow-up Period for an additional 18 months. Participants did not receive study drug during the Long-term Follow-up Period.

seriousEvents.organSystem: Neoplasms benign, malignant and unspecified (incl cysts and polyps)

certainAgreement.otherDetails: AbbVie requests that any investigator or institution that plans on presenting/publishing results disclosure, provide written notification of their request 60 days prior to their presentation/publication. AbbVie requests that no presentation/publication will be instituted until 12 months after a study is completed, or after the first presentation/publication whichever occurs first. A delay may be proposed of a presentation/publication if AbbVie needs to secure patent or proprietary protection.

ancestors.term: Female Urogenital Diseases and Pregnancy Complications
